posts.title: Two Days in the Kiln
posts.excerpt: Fourteen pieces went into the kiln. Eleven came out the way I hoped.
posts.body:
The kiln ran for two days. I never fully relax when it’s firing, half-listening for the sound of it settling.

Inside the kiln, heat has the final say. Some surfaces soften, some colors deepen, and some pieces come out carrying something you couldn’t have planned.

Eleven pieces made it into this edition. The rest get the hammer and return to clay.

posts.title: The numbers
posts.excerpt: A glaze is a set of decisions, and then a long act of letting go.
posts.body:
I mix small batches. Iron oxide, a little ash, water. The numbers matter until the flame decides they don't.

What I'm after isn't a colour so much as a depth, somewhere to look into, not just at.

posts.title: A week of cups
posts.excerpt: Thirty of the same form. By the last one, your hands stop thinking.
posts.body:
Repetition is how the hand learns. The thirtieth cup is quieter than the first, with less effort in it and more ease.

I keep a few from every run. Not the best ones, necessarily. The honest ones.
